#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""RFT Stage-0 headroom probe (docs/grpo_viability_investigation.md §4, Stage 0).

The go/no-go gate that decides whether rejection-sampling fine-tuning (RFT) can
work on Cat A BEFORE spending a training run. For each prompt it generates one
greedy completion and N sampled completions, scores all of them with the
existing graded reward (``reward_business_logic``), and measures how much
headroom best-of-N has over greedy:

    headroom(prompt) = max(sampled_rewards) - greedy_reward

RFT can only distill what best-of-N beats greedy on, so ``frontier_frac`` (the
fraction of prompts with headroom above a threshold) is the direct predictor of
whether RFT has anything to learn. The same run also re-measures GRPO's
admissibility condition (``frac_collapsed_groups`` / median ``reward_std``) at
matched sampling settings, so one probe answers both branches.

Gates (docs/grpo_viability_investigation.md §4):
  GO_RFT       : frontier_frac >= 0.15 AND mean_headroom >= 0.03
  GRPO_REVIVAL : frac_collapsed_groups < 0.50 AND median reward_std >= 0.05
  NO_GO        : frontier_frac < 0.10  (kill the single-turn RL/RFT track)
  MARGINAL     : none of the above (inspect the histogram; likely re-probe)

Reuses the GPU-tested generation + scoring path from preflight_entropy_diag.py.
The reduction (summarize_headroom) and the gate (classify_gate) are pure and
unit-tested in tests/unit/test_rft_headroom.py.

Usage:
    .venv-train/bin/python scripts/rft_headroom_probe.py \
        --checkpoint checkpoints/sft_cat_a/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it/checkpoint-1000 \
        --data-dir data/output/grpo/task_a --split train \
        --n-prompts 500 --n-completions 8 --temperature 0.8 \
        --output runs/preflight/rft_headroom_ckpt1000.json
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import statistics
import sys
import time
from collections import Counter
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
SRC = REPO_ROOT / "src"
if str(SRC) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(SRC))
if str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent))

# Gate thresholds (docs/grpo_viability_investigation.md §4)
FRONTIER_MIN = 0.15
HEADROOM_MIN = 0.03
FRONTIER_NOGO = 0.10
COLLAPSED_MAX = 0.50
REWARD_STD_MIN = 0.05
HEADROOM_THRESHOLD = 0.05  # a prompt is "on the frontier" if headroom exceeds this


def summarize_headroom(
    sampled_rewards: list[list[float]],
    greedy_rewards: list[float],
    headroom_threshold: float = HEADROOM_THRESHOLD,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Reduce per-prompt (sampled rewards, greedy reward) to the probe summary.

    Pure — no model, no I/O. ``sampled_rewards[i]`` is the list of N rewards for
    prompt i; ``greedy_rewards[i]`` is its single greedy reward.
    """
    per_prompt: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    for i, (samps, greedy) in enumerate(zip(sampled_rewards, greedy_rewards)):
        best = max(samps) if samps else 0.0
        headroom = best - greedy
        std = statistics.pstdev(samps) if len(samps) > 1 else 0.0
        n_distinct = len({round(r, 3) for r in samps})
        per_prompt.append({
            "prompt_index": i,
            "greedy_reward": greedy,
            "best_sampled_reward": best,
            "headroom": headroom,
            "reward_std": std,
            "n_distinct_rungs": n_distinct,
            "on_frontier": headroom > headroom_threshold,
        })

    n = len(per_prompt)
    headrooms = [p["headroom"] for p in per_prompt]
    stds = [p["reward_std"] for p in per_prompt]
    return {
        "n_prompts": n,
        "frontier_frac": (sum(p["on_frontier"] for p in per_prompt) / n) if n else 0.0,
        "mean_headroom": statistics.fmean(headrooms) if headrooms else 0.0,
        "median_headroom": statistics.median(headrooms) if headrooms else 0.0,
        "frac_positive_headroom": (
            sum(1 for h in headrooms if h > 0) / n if n else 0.0
        ),
        # GRPO-revival branch metrics (matched-settings re-measurement)
        "frac_collapsed_groups": (
            sum(1 for s in stds if s < 0.01) / n if n else 0.0
        ),
        "median_reward_std": statistics.median(stds) if stds else 0.0,
        "mean_reward_std": statistics.fmean(stds) if stds else 0.0,
        # rung-occupancy histogram: {n_distinct_rungs: n_prompts}
        "rung_histogram": dict(
            sorted(Counter(p["n_distinct_rungs"] for p in per_prompt).items())
        ),
        "per_prompt": per_prompt,
    }


def classify_gate(summary: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
    """Classify the probe summary into a gate verdict. Pure.

    Precedence: GO_RFT > GRPO_REVIVAL > NO_GO > MARGINAL.
    """
    frontier = summary["frontier_frac"]
    mean_hr = summary["mean_headroom"]
    collapsed = summary["frac_collapsed_groups"]
    med_std = summary["median_reward_std"]

    rft_go = frontier >= FRONTIER_MIN and mean_hr >= HEADROOM_MIN
    grpo_revival = collapsed < COLLAPSED_MAX and med_std >= REWARD_STD_MIN
    no_go = frontier < FRONTIER_NOGO

    if rft_go:
        verdict = "GO_RFT"
    elif grpo_revival:
        verdict = "GRPO_REVIVAL"
    elif no_go:
        verdict = "NO_GO"
    else:
        verdict = "MARGINAL"

    return verdict, {
        "rft_go": rft_go,
        "grpo_revival": grpo_revival,
        "no_go": no_go,
        "checks": {
            f"frontier_frac >= {FRONTIER_MIN}": frontier >= FRONTIER_MIN,
            f"mean_headroom >= {HEADROOM_MIN}": mean_hr >= HEADROOM_MIN,
            f"frac_collapsed_groups < {COLLAPSED_MAX}": collapsed < COLLAPSED_MAX,
            f"median_reward_std >= {REWARD_STD_MIN}": med_std >= REWARD_STD_MIN,
            f"frontier_frac < {FRONTIER_NOGO} (no-go)": no_go,
        },
    }


_VERDICT_ACTION = {
    "GO_RFT": "Proceed to Stage 1 — RFT pilot (best-of-8 SFT).",
    "GRPO_REVIVAL": "GRPO condition met at matched settings — run the merged 50-step diagnostic before RFT.",
    "NO_GO": "No single-turn headroom — kill the single-turn RL/RFT track; escalate to multi-turn env or ship SFT-only.",
    "MARGINAL": "Between gates — inspect rung_histogram; likely re-probe with more prompts or a different base checkpoint.",
}


def _score(completions_per_prompt: list[list[str]], gts: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[list[float]]:
    from llm_workflow_agents.training.rewards.reward_business_logic import (
        reward_business_logic,
    )

    out: list[list[float]] = []
    for comps, gt in zip(completions_per_prompt, gts):
        out.append(
            reward_business_logic(
                prompts=[""] * len(comps),
                completions=comps,
                ground_truths=[gt] * len(comps),
            )
        )
    return out


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    parser.add_argument("--checkpoint", required=True, help="SFT checkpoint to probe.")
    parser.add_argument("--data-dir", type=Path, default=Path("data/output/grpo/task_a"))
    parser.add_argument("--split", default="train")
    parser.add_argument("--n-prompts", type=int, default=500)
    parser.add_argument("--n-completions", type=int, default=8)
    parser.add_argument("--max-new-tokens", type=int, default=512)
    parser.add_argument("--temperature", type=float, default=0.8)
    parser.add_argument("--top-p", type=float, default=0.95)
    parser.add_argument("--max-seq-length", type=int, default=8192)
    parser.add_argument("--batch-size", type=int, default=4)
    parser.add_argument("--seed", type=int, default=42)
    parser.add_argument("--output", type=Path, required=True)
    args = parser.parse_args()

    args.output.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    from preflight_entropy_diag import (
        _decode_gt,
        _generate_for_checkpoint,
        _sample_prompts,
    )

    prompts = _sample_prompts(args.data_dir, args.split, args.n_prompts, args.seed)
    gts = [_decode_gt(p["ground_truth"]) for p in prompts]
    print(f"[data] sampled {len(prompts)} prompts from {args.data_dir}/{args.split}.jsonl",
          flush=True)

    gen_kwargs = dict(
        checkpoint=args.checkpoint,
        prompts=prompts,
        max_new_tokens=args.max_new_tokens,
        temperature=args.temperature,
        top_p=args.top_p,
        max_seq_length=args.max_seq_length,
        batch_size=args.batch_size,
        seed=args.seed,
    )

    t0 = time.time()
    print("[gen] greedy pass (do_sample=False)", flush=True)
    greedy = _generate_for_checkpoint(n_completions=1, do_sample=False, **gen_kwargs)
    greedy_texts = [c[0] if c else "" for c in greedy]
    print(f"[gen] sampled pass (N={args.n_completions}, T={args.temperature})", flush=True)
    sampled = _generate_for_checkpoint(
        n_completions=args.n_completions, do_sample=True, **gen_kwargs
    )

    greedy_rewards = _score([[t] for t in greedy_texts], gts)
    greedy_scalar = [r[0] for r in greedy_rewards]
    sampled_rewards = _score(sampled, gts)

    summary = summarize_headroom(sampled_rewards, greedy_scalar)
    summary["wall_time_s"] = round(time.time() - t0, 1)
    verdict, detail = classify_gate(summary)

    results = {
        "config": {
            "checkpoint": args.checkpoint,
            "data": f"{args.data_dir}/{args.split}.jsonl",
            "n_prompts": summary["n_prompts"],
            "n_completions": args.n_completions,
            "temperature": args.temperature,
            "top_p": args.top_p,
            "seed": args.seed,
            "headroom_threshold": HEADROOM_THRESHOLD,
        },
        "summary": {k: v for k, v in summary.items() if k != "per_prompt"},
        "gate": {"verdict": verdict, **detail, "action": _VERDICT_ACTION[verdict]},
        "per_prompt": summary["per_prompt"],
    }
    args.output.write_text(json.dumps(results, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))

    print(
        f"\n=== RFT headroom probe — {Path(args.checkpoint).name} ===\n"
        f"  frontier_frac         = {summary['frontier_frac']:.3f}  (GO_RFT >= {FRONTIER_MIN})\n"
        f"  mean_headroom         = {summary['mean_headroom']:.4f}  (GO_RFT >= {HEADROOM_MIN})\n"
        f"  frac_collapsed_groups = {summary['frac_collapsed_groups']:.3f}  (GRPO revival < {COLLAPSED_MAX})\n"
        f"  median_reward_std     = {summary['median_reward_std']:.4f}  (GRPO revival >= {REWARD_STD_MIN})\n"
        f"  rung_histogram        = {summary['rung_histogram']}\n"
        f"  wall_time_s           = {summary['wall_time_s']}\n"
        f"\n  VERDICT: {verdict} — {_VERDICT_ACTION[verdict]}\n"
        f"[done] wrote {args.output}",
        flush=True,
    )
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Trajectory GRPO Stage-0 variance probe.
3	
4	The go/no-go gate that decides whether the multi-turn *trajectory* reward
5	actually manufactures within-group reward variance BEFORE spending a training
6	run (design spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-09-multiturn-trajectory-reward-design.md
7	§4). For each held-out conversation it duplicates the gold script N times, runs
8	the in-process replay rollout (free-run + truncate-on-divergence), scores each
9	trajectory with ``reward_business_logic_trajectory``, and measures whether the N
10	trajectory rewards spread out — the exact ``reward_std`` that collapsed to ~0 on
11	the per-turn reward and killed four runs.
12	
13	Gates (design spec §4):
14	  GO_TRAJECTORY     : median_reward_std >= 0.05 AND frac_collapsed_groups < 0.50
15	                      AND mean_model_turns >= 3.0 AND 0.05 < mean_coverage < 0.95
16	  NO_GO_VARIANCE    : median_reward_std < 0.02  (trajectory lattice still collapses)
17	  NO_GO_TRUNCATION  : mean_model_turns < 2.0    (over-truncation re-collapses variance)
18	  MARGINAL          : none of the above (inspect histograms; re-probe)
19	
20	The reduction (summarize_trajectory_probe) and the gate (classify_gate) are pure
21	and unit-tested in tests/unit/test_trajectory_probe.py. ``main`` is train-box
22	only (needs the H100 + Unsloth + trl==1.0.0).
23	
24	Usage (H100 / .venv-train):
25	    .venv-train/bin/python scripts/trajectory_variance_probe.py \
26	        --checkpoint checkpoints/sft_cat_a/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it/checkpoint-1000 \
27	        --data-dir data/output/grpo/task_a --split validation \
28	        --n-conversations 50 --n-completions 8 --temperature 0.8 --top-p 0.95 \
29	        --output runs/preflight/trajectory_variance_probe.json
30	"""
31	
32	from __future__ import annotations
33	
34	import argparse
35	import json
36	import random
37	import statistics
38	import sys
39	import time
40	from collections import Counter
41	from pathlib import Path
42	from typing import Any
43	
44	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
45	SRC = REPO_ROOT / "src"
46	if str(SRC) not in sys.path:
47	    sys.path.insert(0, str(SRC))
48	if str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent) not in sys.path:
49	    sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent))
50	
51	# Gate thresholds (design spec §4)
52	REWARD_STD_GO = 0.05
53	COLLAPSED_MAX = 0.50
54	TURNS_GO = 3.0
55	COVERAGE_LO = 0.05
56	COVERAGE_HI = 0.95
57	REWARD_STD_NOGO = 0.02
58	TURNS_NOGO = 2.0
59	COLLAPSE_STD_EPS = 0.01  # a group is "collapsed" if its reward std < this
60	
61	
62	def summarize_trajectory_probe(
63	    group_rewards: list[list[float]],
64	    group_coverages: list[list[float]],
65	    group_metas: list[list[dict[str, Any]]],
66	) -> dict[str, Any]:
67	    """Reduce per-conversation (rewards, coverages, metas) to the probe summary.
68	
69	    Pure — no model, no I/O. ``group_rewards[i]`` is the list of N trajectory
70	    rewards for conversation i (one per sampled rollout); ``group_coverages[i]``
71	    and ``group_metas[i]`` are the matching per-rollout coverage fractions and
72	    rollout meta dicts.
73	    """
74	    per_group: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
75	    all_coverages: list[float] = []
76	    all_turns: list[int] = []
77	    stop_reasons: Counter = Counter()
78	
79	    for i, (rewards, covs, metas) in enumerate(
80	        zip(group_rewards, group_coverages, group_metas)
81	    ):
82	        std = statistics.pstdev(rewards) if len(rewards) > 1 else 0.0
83	        spread = (max(covs) - min(covs)) if covs else 0.0
84	        n_distinct = len({round(r, 3) for r in rewards})
85	        per_group.append(
86	            {
87	                "group_index": i,
88	                "reward_std": std,
89	                "coverage_spread": spread,
90	                "n_distinct_rungs": n_distinct,
91	                "collapsed": std < COLLAPSE_STD_EPS,
92	            }
93	        )
94	        all_coverages.extend(covs)
95	        all_turns.extend(int(m.get("n_model_turns", 0)) for m in metas)
96	        stop_reasons.update(m.get("stop_reason", "unknown") for m in metas)
97	
98	    n = len(per_group)
99	    stds = [g["reward_std"] for g in per_group]
100	    spreads = [g["coverage_spread"] for g in per_group]
101	    sorted_cov = sorted(all_coverages)
102	
103	    def _pct(p: float) -> float:
104	        if not sorted_cov:
105	            return 0.0
106	        idx = min(len(sorted_cov) - 1, max(0, int(round(p * (len(sorted_cov) - 1)))))
107	        return sorted_cov[idx]
108	
109	    return {
110	        "n_groups": n,
111	        "median_reward_std": statistics.median(stds) if stds else 0.0,
112	        "mean_reward_std": statistics.fmean(stds) if stds else 0.0,
113	        "frac_collapsed_groups": (
114	            sum(1 for g in per_group if g["collapsed"]) / n if n else 0.0
115	        ),
116	        "mean_coverage": statistics.fmean(all_coverages) if all_coverages else 0.0,
117	        "coverage_p10": _pct(0.10),
118	        "coverage_p90": _pct(0.90),
119	        "mean_within_group_coverage_spread": (
120	            statistics.fmean(spreads) if spreads else 0.0
121	        ),
122	        "mean_model_turns": statistics.fmean(all_turns) if all_turns else 0.0,
123	        "stop_reason_histogram": dict(sorted(stop_reasons.items())),
124	        "rung_histogram": dict(
125	            sorted(Counter(g["n_distinct_rungs"] for g in per_group).items())
126	        ),
127	        "per_group": per_group,
128	    }
129	
130	
131	def classify_gate(summary: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
132	    """Classify the probe summary into a gate verdict. Pure.
133	
134	    Precedence: GO_TRAJECTORY > NO_GO_VARIANCE > NO_GO_TRUNCATION > MARGINAL.
135	    """
136	    med_std = summary["median_reward_std"]
137	    collapsed = summary["frac_collapsed_groups"]
138	    turns = summary["mean_model_turns"]
139	    cov = summary["mean_coverage"]
140	
141	    go = (
142	        med_std >= REWARD_STD_GO
143	        and collapsed < COLLAPSED_MAX
144	        and turns >= TURNS_GO
145	        and COVERAGE_LO < cov < COVERAGE_HI
146	    )
147	    if go:
148	        verdict = "GO_TRAJECTORY"
149	    elif med_std < REWARD_STD_NOGO:
150	        verdict = "NO_GO_VARIANCE"
151	    elif turns < TURNS_NOGO:
152	        verdict = "NO_GO_TRUNCATION"
153	    else:
154	        verdict = "MARGINAL"
155	
156	    return verdict, {
157	        "checks": {
158	            f"median_reward_std >= {REWARD_STD_GO}": med_std >= REWARD_STD_GO,
159	            f"frac_collapsed_groups < {COLLAPSED_MAX}": collapsed < COLLAPSED_MAX,
160	            f"mean_model_turns >= {TURNS_GO}": turns >= TURNS_GO,
161	            f"{COVERAGE_LO} < mean_coverage < {COVERAGE_HI}": (
162	                COVERAGE_LO < cov < COVERAGE_HI
163	            ),
164	            f"median_reward_std < {REWARD_STD_NOGO} (no-go)": med_std < REWARD_STD_NOGO,
165	            f"mean_model_turns < {TURNS_NOGO} (no-go)": turns < TURNS_NOGO,
166	        },
167	    }
168	
169	
170	_VERDICT_ACTION = {
171	    "GO_TRAJECTORY": "Trajectory reward manufactures variance — build Task 6 trainer wiring and run the 50-step diagnostic.",
172	    "NO_GO_VARIANCE": "Trajectory lattice still collapses — abandon multi-turn; fall back to RFT/SFT-only (spec §7).",
173	    "NO_GO_TRUNCATION": "Over-truncation floors trajectory length — relax stall_turn_limit (2->3) / one-legal-edge tolerance and re-probe, else abandon (spec §7).",
174	    "MARGINAL": "Between gates — inspect histograms; re-probe with more conversations.",
175	}
176	
177	
178	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
179	# GPU driver (train box only).
180	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
181	
182	
183	def _sample_conversations(
184	    data_dir: Path, split: str, n: int, seed: int
185	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
186	    """Read the per-conversation JSONL and sample ``n`` raw conversation rows."""
187	    path = Path(data_dir) / f"{split}.jsonl"
188	    rows = [json.loads(line) for line in path.read_text().splitlines() if line.strip()]
189	    rng = random.Random(seed)
190	    if len(rows) > n:
191	        rows = rng.sample(rows, n)
192	    return rows
193	
194	
195	def _build_scripts(conversations: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[Any]:
196	    """Build GoldScripts, enriching the system prompt like the GRPO loader."""
197	    from llm_workflow_agents.data.system_prompt import build_enriched_system_prompt
198	    from llm_workflow_agents.training.trajectory_rollout import build_gold_script
199	
200	    scripts = []
201	    for raw in conversations:
202	        msgs = raw.get("messages", [])
203	        if msgs and msgs[0].get("role") == "system" and raw.get("workflow_graph"):
204	            enriched = build_enriched_system_prompt(
205	                raw, msgs[0].get("content") or "", force_rebuild=True
206	            )
207	        elif msgs and msgs[0].get("role") == "system":
208	            enriched = msgs[0].get("content") or ""
209	        else:
210	            enriched = ""
211	        try:
212	            scripts.append(build_gold_script(raw, enriched))
213	        except ValueError as exc:  # invariant violation / no assistant turns
214	            print(f"[skip] {raw.get('conversation_id')}: {exc}", flush=True)
215	    return scripts
216	
217	
218	def _load_model_and_tokenizer(checkpoint: str, max_seq_length: int):  # noqa: ANN201
219	    """Load the SFT checkpoint for inference (mirrors preflight_entropy_diag).
220	
221	    Uses the *same* proven Gemma-4 workaround as the headroom probe rather than
222	    grpo.py's ``_unwrap_unsloth_gemma4_kv_zero_proxy``. The unwrap strips the
223	    KV-zero proxy off ``get_text_config`` returns, but Gemma-4 26B-A4B load
224	    trips a different surface: ``Gemma4Config.__post_init__`` constructs a
225	    ``Gemma4TextConfig`` whose transformers 5.x ``validate_token_ids`` iterates
226	    the config and raw-``getattr``s ``num_kv_shared_layers`` on the proxy,
227	    hitting its raise. The proxy must stay alive (cache ``__init__`` relies on
228	    ``hasattr(...) == False``), so we filter that name out of the proxy's
229	    ``__iter__`` instead — the fix that lets the headroom probe load this same
230	    checkpoint. Order matters: import unsloth first (it installs the proxy),
231	    patch after.
232	    """
233	    from unsloth import FastLanguageModel
234	
235	    from preflight_entropy_diag import _patch_unsloth_gemma4_proxy_iter
236	
237	    _patch_unsloth_gemma4_proxy_iter()
238	
239	    model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(
240	        model_name=checkpoint,
241	        max_seq_length=max_seq_length,
242	        dtype=None,
243	        load_in_4bit=True,
244	    )
245	    FastLanguageModel.for_inference(model)
246	
247	    # Gemma-4 loads as a multimodal ``Gemma4Processor``, not a bare tokenizer.
248	    # Its ``apply_chat_template(..., tokenize=True)`` returns a *batched* (nested)
249	    # id list, so ``run_replay_rollout``'s ``_derive_turn_end_id`` /
250	    # ``_segment_suffix_ids`` (which expect a flat ``list[int]``) break with
251	    # "int() argument must be ... not 'list'". The inner ``.tokenizer`` is the
252	    # correct object for text tokenization (the processor wrapper only adds the
253	    # image path) and returns flat ids — same resolution the headroom probe uses.
254	    inner_tok = getattr(tokenizer, "tokenizer", tokenizer)
255	    return model, inner_tok
256	
257	
258	def main() -> int:
259	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
260	    parser.add_argument("--checkpoint", required=True, help="SFT checkpoint to probe.")
261	    parser.add_argument(
262	        "--data-dir", type=Path, default=Path("data/output/grpo/task_a")
263	    )
264	    parser.add_argument("--split", default="validation")
265	    parser.add_argument("--n-conversations", type=int, default=50)
266	    parser.add_argument("--n-completions", type=int, default=8)
267	    parser.add_argument("--temperature", type=float, default=0.8)
268	    parser.add_argument("--top-p", type=float, default=0.95)
269	    parser.add_argument("--max-seq-length", type=int, default=8192)
270	    parser.add_argument("--max-turns", type=int, default=24)
271	    parser.add_argument("--per-turn-max-new-tokens", type=int, default=256)
272	    parser.add_argument("--max-completion-tokens", type=int, default=4096)
273	    parser.add_argument("--stall-turn-limit", type=int, default=2)
274	    parser.add_argument("--seed", type=int, default=42)
275	    parser.add_argument("--output", type=Path, required=True)
276	    args = parser.parse_args()
277	
278	    args.output.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
279	
280	    from llm_workflow_agents.training.rewards.reward_business_logic import (
281	        reward_business_logic_trajectory,
282	    )
283	    from llm_workflow_agents.training.trajectory_rollout import (
284	        TrajectoryRolloutConfig,
285	        assert_trajectory_rollout_support,
286	        run_replay_rollout,
287	    )
288	
289	    assert_trajectory_rollout_support()
290	
291	    conversations = _sample_conversations(
292	        args.data_dir, args.split, args.n_conversations, args.seed
293	    )
294	    scripts = _build_scripts(conversations)
295	    print(f"[data] {len(scripts)} scripts from {args.data_dir}/{args.split}.jsonl",
296	          flush=True)
297	
298	    model, tokenizer = _load_model_and_tokenizer(args.checkpoint, args.max_seq_length)
299	    cfg = TrajectoryRolloutConfig(
300	        max_turns=args.max_turns,
301	        per_turn_max_new_tokens=args.per_turn_max_new_tokens,
302	        max_completion_tokens=args.max_completion_tokens,
303	        stall_turn_limit=args.stall_turn_limit,
304	        temperature=args.temperature,
305	        top_p=args.top_p,
306	        do_sample=True,
307	    )
308	
309	    t0 = time.time()
310	    group_rewards: list[list[float]] = []
311	    group_coverages: list[list[float]] = []
312	    group_metas: list[list[dict[str, Any]]] = []
313	    mask_audit: dict[str, Any] | None = None
314	
315	    for gi, sc in enumerate(scripts):
316	        samples = run_replay_rollout(model, tokenizer, [sc] * args.n_completions, cfg)
317	        gts = [
318	            {
319	                "state_sequence": [
320	                    {"from": f, "to": t} for (f, t) in sc.gold_transitions
321	                ],
322	                "tool_calls": sc.gold_tool_calls,
323	                "terminal_state": sc.terminal_state,
324	                "terminal_reached": sc.terminal_reached,
325	                "valid_transitions": sc.valid_transitions,
326	            }
327	            for _ in samples
328	        ]
329	        rewards = reward_business_logic_trajectory(
330	            [None] * len(samples),
331	            [s.turn_texts for s in samples],
332	            [s.meta for s in samples],
333	            gts,
334	        )
335	        group_rewards.append(rewards)
336	        group_coverages.append(
337	            [s.meta["cursor"] / max(s.meta["gold_len"], 1) for s in samples]
338	        )
339	        group_metas.append([s.meta for s in samples])
340	
341	        if mask_audit is None and samples:  # step-0 mask audit on the first group
342	            s0 = samples[0]
343	            mask_audit = {
344	                "len_match": len(s0.env_mask) == len(s0.completion_ids),
345	                "model_frac": (
346	                    sum(s0.env_mask) / len(s0.env_mask) if s0.env_mask else 0.0
347	                ),
348	                "ends_on_eos": bool(
349	                    s0.completion_ids
350	                    and s0.completion_ids[-1] == int(tokenizer.eos_token_id)
351	                ),
352	            }
353	        if (gi + 1) % 10 == 0:
354	            print(f"[gen] {gi + 1}/{len(scripts)} conversations", flush=True)
355	
356	    summary = summarize_trajectory_probe(group_rewards, group_coverages, group_metas)
357	    summary["wall_time_s"] = round(time.time() - t0, 1)
358	    verdict, detail = classify_gate(summary)
359	
360	    results = {
361	        "config": {
362	            "checkpoint": args.checkpoint,
363	            "data": f"{args.data_dir}/{args.split}.jsonl",
364	            "n_conversations": summary["n_groups"],
365	            "n_completions": args.n_completions,
366	            "temperature": args.temperature,
367	            "top_p": args.top_p,
368	            "seed": args.seed,
369	        },
370	        "summary": {k: v for k, v in summary.items() if k != "per_group"},
371	        "mask_audit": mask_audit,
372	        "gate": {"verdict": verdict, **detail, "action": _VERDICT_ACTION[verdict]},
373	        "per_group": summary["per_group"],
374	    }
375	    args.output.write_text(json.dumps(results, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))
376	
377	    print(
378	        f"\n=== trajectory variance probe — {Path(args.checkpoint).name} ===\n"
379	        f"  median_reward_std     = {summary['median_reward_std']:.4f}  (GO >= {REWARD_STD_GO})\n"
380	        f"  frac_collapsed_groups = {summary['frac_collapsed_groups']:.3f}  (GO < {COLLAPSED_MAX})\n"
381	        f"  mean_model_turns      = {summary['mean_model_turns']:.2f}  (GO >= {TURNS_GO})\n"
382	        f"  mean_coverage         = {summary['mean_coverage']:.3f}  (GO in {COVERAGE_LO}-{COVERAGE_HI})\n"
383	        f"  stop_reasons          = {summary['stop_reason_histogram']}\n"
384	        f"  rung_histogram        = {summary['rung_histogram']}\n"
385	        f"  mask_audit            = {mask_audit}\n"
386	        f"  wall_time_s           = {summary['wall_time_s']}\n"
387	        f"\n  VERDICT: {verdict} — {_VERDICT_ACTION[verdict]}\n"
388	        f"[done] wrote {args.output}",
389	        flush=True,
390	    )
391	    return 0
392	
393	
394	if __name__ == "__main__":
395	    raise SystemExit(main())

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Trajectory GRPO Stage-0 variance probe.

The go/no-go gate that decides whether the multi-turn *trajectory* reward
actually manufactures within-group reward variance BEFORE spending a training
run (design spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-09-multiturn-trajectory-reward-design.md
§4). For each held-out conversation it duplicates the gold script N times, runs
the in-process replay rollout (free-run + truncate-on-divergence), scores each
trajectory with ``reward_business_logic_trajectory``, and measures whether the N
trajectory rewards spread out — the exact ``reward_std`` that collapsed to ~0 on
the per-turn reward and killed four runs.

Gates (design spec §4):
  GO_TRAJECTORY     : median_reward_std >= 0.05 AND frac_collapsed_groups < 0.50
                      AND mean_model_turns >= 3.0 AND 0.05 < mean_coverage < 0.95
  NO_GO_VARIANCE    : median_reward_std < 0.02  (trajectory lattice still collapses)
  NO_GO_TRUNCATION  : mean_model_turns < 2.0    (over-truncation re-collapses variance)
  MARGINAL          : none of the above (inspect histograms; re-probe)

The reduction (summarize_trajectory_probe) and the gate (classify_gate) are pure
and unit-tested in tests/unit/test_trajectory_probe.py. ``main`` is train-box
only (needs the H100 + Unsloth + trl==1.0.0).

Usage (H100 / .venv-train):
    .venv-train/bin/python scripts/trajectory_variance_probe.py \
        --checkpoint checkpoints/sft_cat_a/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it/checkpoint-1000 \
        --data-dir data/output/grpo/task_a --split validation \
        --n-conversations 50 --n-completions 8 --temperature 0.8 --top-p 0.95 \
        --output runs/preflight/trajectory_variance_probe.json
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import json
import random
import statistics
import sys
import time
from collections import Counter
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
SRC = REPO_ROOT / "src"
if str(SRC) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(SRC))
if str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent) not in sys.path:
    sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent))

# Gate thresholds (design spec §4)
REWARD_STD_GO = 0.05
COLLAPSED_MAX = 0.50
TURNS_GO = 3.0
COVERAGE_LO = 0.05
COVERAGE_HI = 0.95
REWARD_STD_NOGO = 0.02
TURNS_NOGO = 2.0
COLLAPSE_STD_EPS = 0.01  # a group is "collapsed" if its reward std < this


def summarize_trajectory_probe(
    group_rewards: list[list[float]],
    group_coverages: list[list[float]],
    group_metas: list[list[dict[str, Any]]],
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Reduce per-conversation (rewards, coverages, metas) to the probe summary.

    Pure — no model, no I/O. ``group_rewards[i]`` is the list of N trajectory
    rewards for conversation i (one per sampled rollout); ``group_coverages[i]``
    and ``group_metas[i]`` are the matching per-rollout coverage fractions and
    rollout meta dicts.
    """
    per_group: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    all_coverages: list[float] = []
    all_turns: list[int] = []
    stop_reasons: Counter = Counter()

    for i, (rewards, covs, metas) in enumerate(
        zip(group_rewards, group_coverages, group_metas)
    ):
        std = statistics.pstdev(rewards) if len(rewards) > 1 else 0.0
        spread = (max(covs) - min(covs)) if covs else 0.0
        n_distinct = len({round(r, 3) for r in rewards})
        per_group.append(
            {
                "group_index": i,
                "reward_std": std,
                "coverage_spread": spread,
                "n_distinct_rungs": n_distinct,
                "collapsed": std < COLLAPSE_STD_EPS,
            }
        )
        all_coverages.extend(covs)
        all_turns.extend(int(m.get("n_model_turns", 0)) for m in metas)
        stop_reasons.update(m.get("stop_reason", "unknown") for m in metas)

    n = len(per_group)
    stds = [g["reward_std"] for g in per_group]
    spreads = [g["coverage_spread"] for g in per_group]
    sorted_cov = sorted(all_coverages)

    def _pct(p: float) -> float:
        if not sorted_cov:
            return 0.0
        idx = min(len(sorted_cov) - 1, max(0, int(round(p * (len(sorted_cov) - 1)))))
        return sorted_cov[idx]

    return {
        "n_groups": n,
        "median_reward_std": statistics.median(stds) if stds else 0.0,
        "mean_reward_std": statistics.fmean(stds) if stds else 0.0,
        "frac_collapsed_groups": (
            sum(1 for g in per_group if g["collapsed"]) / n if n else 0.0
        ),
        "mean_coverage": statistics.fmean(all_coverages) if all_coverages else 0.0,
        "coverage_p10": _pct(0.10),
        "coverage_p90": _pct(0.90),
        "mean_within_group_coverage_spread": (
            statistics.fmean(spreads) if spreads else 0.0
        ),
        "mean_model_turns": statistics.fmean(all_turns) if all_turns else 0.0,
        "stop_reason_histogram": dict(sorted(stop_reasons.items())),
        "rung_histogram": dict(
            sorted(Counter(g["n_distinct_rungs"] for g in per_group).items())
        ),
        "per_group": per_group,
    }


def classify_gate(summary: dict[str, Any]) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
    """Classify the probe summary into a gate verdict. Pure.

    Precedence: GO_TRAJECTORY > NO_GO_VARIANCE > NO_GO_TRUNCATION > MARGINAL.
    """
    med_std = summary["median_reward_std"]
    collapsed = summary["frac_collapsed_groups"]
    turns = summary["mean_model_turns"]
    cov = summary["mean_coverage"]

    go = (
        med_std >= REWARD_STD_GO
        and collapsed < COLLAPSED_MAX
        and turns >= TURNS_GO
        and COVERAGE_LO < cov < COVERAGE_HI
    )
    if go:
        verdict = "GO_TRAJECTORY"
    elif med_std < REWARD_STD_NOGO:
        verdict = "NO_GO_VARIANCE"
    elif turns < TURNS_NOGO:
        verdict = "NO_GO_TRUNCATION"
    else:
        verdict = "MARGINAL"

    return verdict, {
        "checks": {
            f"median_reward_std >= {REWARD_STD_GO}": med_std >= REWARD_STD_GO,
            f"frac_collapsed_groups < {COLLAPSED_MAX}": collapsed < COLLAPSED_MAX,
            f"mean_model_turns >= {TURNS_GO}": turns >= TURNS_GO,
            f"{COVERAGE_LO} < mean_coverage < {COVERAGE_HI}": (
                COVERAGE_LO < cov < COVERAGE_HI
            ),
            f"median_reward_std < {REWARD_STD_NOGO} (no-go)": med_std < REWARD_STD_NOGO,
            f"mean_model_turns < {TURNS_NOGO} (no-go)": turns < TURNS_NOGO,
        },
    }


_VERDICT_ACTION = {
    "GO_TRAJECTORY": "Trajectory reward manufactures variance — build Task 6 trainer wiring and run the 50-step diagnostic.",
    "NO_GO_VARIANCE": "Trajectory lattice still collapses — abandon multi-turn; fall back to RFT/SFT-only (spec §7).",
    "NO_GO_TRUNCATION": "Over-truncation floors trajectory length — relax stall_turn_limit (2->3) / one-legal-edge tolerance and re-probe, else abandon (spec §7).",
    "MARGINAL": "Between gates — inspect histograms; re-probe with more conversations.",
}


# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
# GPU driver (train box only).
# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #


def _sample_conversations(
    data_dir: Path, split: str, n: int, seed: int
) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """Read the per-conversation JSONL and sample ``n`` raw conversation rows."""
    path = Path(data_dir) / f"{split}.jsonl"
    rows = [json.loads(line) for line in path.read_text().splitlines() if line.strip()]
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    if len(rows) > n:
        rows = rng.sample(rows, n)
    return rows


def _build_scripts(conversations: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[Any]:
    """Build GoldScripts, enriching the system prompt like the GRPO loader."""
    from llm_workflow_agents.data.system_prompt import build_enriched_system_prompt
    from llm_workflow_agents.training.trajectory_rollout import build_gold_script

    scripts = []
    for raw in conversations:
        msgs = raw.get("messages", [])
        if msgs and msgs[0].get("role") == "system" and raw.get("workflow_graph"):
            enriched = build_enriched_system_prompt(
                raw, msgs[0].get("content") or "", force_rebuild=True
            )
        elif msgs and msgs[0].get("role") == "system":
            enriched = msgs[0].get("content") or ""
        else:
            enriched = ""
        try:
            scripts.append(build_gold_script(raw, enriched))
        except ValueError as exc:  # invariant violation / no assistant turns
            print(f"[skip] {raw.get('conversation_id')}: {exc}", flush=True)
    return scripts


def _load_model_and_tokenizer(checkpoint: str, max_seq_length: int):  # noqa: ANN201
    """Load the SFT checkpoint for inference (mirrors preflight_entropy_diag).

    Uses the *same* proven Gemma-4 workaround as the headroom probe rather than
    grpo.py's ``_unwrap_unsloth_gemma4_kv_zero_proxy``. The unwrap strips the
    KV-zero proxy off ``get_text_config`` returns, but Gemma-4 26B-A4B load
    trips a different surface: ``Gemma4Config.__post_init__`` constructs a
    ``Gemma4TextConfig`` whose transformers 5.x ``validate_token_ids`` iterates
    the config and raw-``getattr``s ``num_kv_shared_layers`` on the proxy,
    hitting its raise. The proxy must stay alive (cache ``__init__`` relies on
    ``hasattr(...) == False``), so we filter that name out of the proxy's
    ``__iter__`` instead — the fix that lets the headroom probe load this same
    checkpoint. Order matters: import unsloth first (it installs the proxy),
    patch after.
    """
    from unsloth import FastLanguageModel

    from preflight_entropy_diag import _patch_unsloth_gemma4_proxy_iter

    _patch_unsloth_gemma4_proxy_iter()

    model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(
        model_name=checkpoint,
        max_seq_length=max_seq_length,
        dtype=None,
        load_in_4bit=True,
    )
    FastLanguageModel.for_inference(model)

    # Gemma-4 loads as a multimodal ``Gemma4Processor``, not a bare tokenizer.
    # Its ``apply_chat_template(..., tokenize=True)`` returns a *batched* (nested)
    # id list, so ``run_replay_rollout``'s ``_derive_turn_end_id`` /
    # ``_segment_suffix_ids`` (which expect a flat ``list[int]``) break with
    # "int() argument must be ... not 'list'". The inner ``.tokenizer`` is the
    # correct object for text tokenization (the processor wrapper only adds the
    # image path) and returns flat ids — same resolution the headroom probe uses.
    inner_tok = getattr(tokenizer, "tokenizer", tokenizer)
    return model, inner_tok


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
    parser.add_argument("--checkpoint", required=True, help="SFT checkpoint to probe.")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--data-dir", type=Path, default=Path("data/output/grpo/task_a")
    )
    parser.add_argument("--split", default="validation")
    parser.add_argument("--n-conversations", type=int, default=50)
    parser.add_argument("--n-completions", type=int, default=8)
    parser.add_argument("--temperature", type=float, default=0.8)
    parser.add_argument("--top-p", type=float, default=0.95)
    parser.add_argument("--max-seq-length", type=int, default=8192)
    parser.add_argument("--max-turns", type=int, default=24)
    parser.add_argument("--per-turn-max-new-tokens", type=int, default=256)
    parser.add_argument("--max-completion-tokens", type=int, default=4096)
    parser.add_argument("--stall-turn-limit", type=int, default=2)
    parser.add_argument("--seed", type=int, default=42)
    parser.add_argument("--output", type=Path, required=True)
    args = parser.parse_args()

    args.output.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    from llm_workflow_agents.training.rewards.reward_business_logic import (
        reward_business_logic_trajectory,
    )
    from llm_workflow_agents.training.trajectory_rollout import (
        TrajectoryRolloutConfig,
        assert_trajectory_rollout_support,
        run_replay_rollout,
    )

    assert_trajectory_rollout_support()

    conversations = _sample_conversations(
        args.data_dir, args.split, args.n_conversations, args.seed
    )
    scripts = _build_scripts(conversations)
    print(f"[data] {len(scripts)} scripts from {args.data_dir}/{args.split}.jsonl",
          flush=True)

    model, tokenizer = _load_model_and_tokenizer(args.checkpoint, args.max_seq_length)
    cfg = TrajectoryRolloutConfig(
        max_turns=args.max_turns,
        per_turn_max_new_tokens=args.per_turn_max_new_tokens,
        max_completion_tokens=args.max_completion_tokens,
        stall_turn_limit=args.stall_turn_limit,
        temperature=args.temperature,
        top_p=args.top_p,
        do_sample=True,
    )

    t0 = time.time()
    group_rewards: list[list[float]] = []
    group_coverages: list[list[float]] = []
    group_metas: list[list[dict[str, Any]]] = []
    mask_audit: dict[str, Any] | None = None

    for gi, sc in enumerate(scripts):
        samples = run_replay_rollout(model, tokenizer, [sc] * args.n_completions, cfg)
        gts = [
            {
                "state_sequence": [
                    {"from": f, "to": t} for (f, t) in sc.gold_transitions
                ],
                "tool_calls": sc.gold_tool_calls,
                "terminal_state": sc.terminal_state,
                "terminal_reached": sc.terminal_reached,
                "valid_transitions": sc.valid_transitions,
            }
            for _ in samples
        ]
        rewards = reward_business_logic_trajectory(
            [None] * len(samples),
            [s.turn_texts for s in samples],
            [s.meta for s in samples],
            gts,
        )
        group_rewards.append(rewards)
        group_coverages.append(
            [s.meta["cursor"] / max(s.meta["gold_len"], 1) for s in samples]
        )
        group_metas.append([s.meta for s in samples])

        if mask_audit is None and samples:  # step-0 mask audit on the first group
            s0 = samples[0]
            mask_audit = {
                "len_match": len(s0.env_mask) == len(s0.completion_ids),
                "model_frac": (
                    sum(s0.env_mask) / len(s0.env_mask) if s0.env_mask else 0.0
                ),
                "ends_on_eos": bool(
                    s0.completion_ids
                    and s0.completion_ids[-1] == int(tokenizer.eos_token_id)
                ),
            }
        if (gi + 1) % 10 == 0:
            print(f"[gen] {gi + 1}/{len(scripts)} conversations", flush=True)

    summary = summarize_trajectory_probe(group_rewards, group_coverages, group_metas)
    summary["wall_time_s"] = round(time.time() - t0, 1)
    verdict, detail = classify_gate(summary)

    results = {
        "config": {
            "checkpoint": args.checkpoint,
            "data": f"{args.data_dir}/{args.split}.jsonl",
            "n_conversations": summary["n_groups"],
            "n_completions": args.n_completions,
            "temperature": args.temperature,
            "top_p": args.top_p,
            "seed": args.seed,
        },
        "summary": {k: v for k, v in summary.items() if k != "per_group"},
        "mask_audit": mask_audit,
        "gate": {"verdict": verdict, **detail, "action": _VERDICT_ACTION[verdict]},
        "per_group": summary["per_group"],
    }
    args.output.write_text(json.dumps(results, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))

    print(
        f"\n=== trajectory variance probe — {Path(args.checkpoint).name} ===\n"
        f"  median_reward_std     = {summary['median_reward_std']:.4f}  (GO >= {REWARD_STD_GO})\n"
        f"  frac_collapsed_groups = {summary['frac_collapsed_groups']:.3f}  (GO < {COLLAPSED_MAX})\n"
        f"  mean_model_turns      = {summary['mean_model_turns']:.2f}  (GO >= {TURNS_GO})\n"
        f"  mean_coverage         = {summary['mean_coverage']:.3f}  (GO in {COVERAGE_LO}-{COVERAGE_HI})\n"
        f"  stop_reasons          = {summary['stop_reason_histogram']}\n"
        f"  rung_histogram        = {summary['rung_histogram']}\n"
        f"  mask_audit            = {mask_audit}\n"
        f"  wall_time_s           = {summary['wall_time_s']}\n"
        f"\n  VERDICT: {verdict} — {_VERDICT_ACTION[verdict]}\n"
        f"[done] wrote {args.output}",
        flush=True,
    )
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Pre-flight entropy diagnostic for GRPO restart (step 3 of grpo_diagnosis_gemma4_26b.md).
3	
4	Compares two SFT checkpoints by generating K rollouts per prompt at GRPO
5	sampling settings (T=1.0, top_p=0.95) and reporting per-prompt reward_std —
6	the quantity GRPO actually consumes as advantage signal. If checkpoint-500
7	shows materially higher reward_std than checkpoint-1656, the SFT-too-good
8	hypothesis from the diagnosis is confirmed.
9	
10	Usage:
11	    python scripts/preflight_entropy_diag.py \
12	        --checkpoints \
13	            checkpoints/sft_cat_a/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it/checkpoint-500 \
14	            checkpoints/sft_cat_a/gemma-4-26B-A4B-it/checkpoint-1656 \
15	        --n-prompts 5 --n-completions 4 \
16	        --output runs/preflight/smoke.json
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import argparse
22	import gc
23	import json
24	import random
25	import statistics
26	import sys
27	import time
28	from pathlib import Path
29	from typing import Any
30	
31	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
32	SRC = REPO_ROOT / "src"
33	if str(SRC) not in sys.path:
34	    sys.path.insert(0, str(SRC))
35	
36	
37	def _sample_prompts(
38	    data_dir: Path,
39	    split: str,
40	    n_prompts: int,
41	    seed: int,
42	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
43	    """Slice validation conversations into GRPO-format rows, then pick N unique prompts.
44	
45	    Uses the exact slicer GRPO sees (one row per user→assistant boundary),
46	    then dedupes by conversation_id to avoid picking adjacent turns of the
47	    same conversation — those would inflate apparent diversity differences
48	    via prompt similarity, not policy entropy.
49	    """
50	    from llm_workflow_agents.training.grpo import _load_grpo_jsonl
51	
52	    ds = _load_grpo_jsonl(data_dir, split=split)
53	    # _load_grpo_jsonl doesn't carry conversation_id; key on the rendered
54	    # system+user prefix instead. Two rows from the same conversation share
55	    # the same first user message, so this dedupes correctly.
56	    rng = random.Random(seed)
57	    indices = list(range(len(ds)))
58	    rng.shuffle(indices)
59	
60	    chosen: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
61	    seen_first_user: set[str] = set()
62	    for i in indices:
63	        row = ds[i]
64	        msgs = row["prompt"]
65	        first_user = next(
66	            (m["content"] for m in msgs if m["role"] == "user"), ""
67	        )
68	        key = first_user[:200]
69	        if key in seen_first_user:
70	            continue
71	        seen_first_user.add(key)
72	        chosen.append({
73	            "prompt_messages": row["prompt"],
74	            "ground_truth": row["ground_truth"],
75	        })
76	        if len(chosen) >= n_prompts:
77	            break
78	
79	    if len(chosen) < n_prompts:
80	        print(
81	            f"[warn] only {len(chosen)} unique conversations available "
82	            f"(requested {n_prompts}); proceeding with what we have.",
83	            file=sys.stderr,
84	        )
85	    return chosen
86	
87	
88	def _decode_gt(gt_str: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
89	    """Decode and reshape the GRPO ground_truth JSON the way _make_reward_adapter does."""
90	    d = json.loads(gt_str) if isinstance(gt_str, str) else (gt_str or {})
91	    if not isinstance(d, dict):
92	        d = {}
93	    if "state_sequence" in d and "state_annotations" not in d:
94	        seq = d["state_sequence"]
95	        if isinstance(seq, list):
96	            d["state_annotations"] = [
97	                (s.get("from", ""), s.get("to", "")) if isinstance(s, dict)
98	                else tuple(s) if isinstance(s, (list, tuple)) and len(s) == 2
99	                else ("", "")
100	                for s in seq
101	            ]
102	        else:
103	            d["state_annotations"] = []
104	    return d
105	
106	
107	def _patch_unsloth_gemma4_proxy_iter() -> None:
108	    """Filter ``num_kv_shared_layers`` out of ``_Gemma4KVSharedSafeProxy.__iter__``.
109	
110	    The proxy must stay alive: cache ``__init__`` short-circuits the buggy
111	    ``layer_types[:-0] == []`` slice via ``hasattr(decoder_config,
112	    "num_kv_shared_layers") == False``, which only holds while the proxy's
113	    ``__getattr__`` raises on that name. So we cannot unwrap the proxy.
114	
115	    But transformers 5.9.0's strict-dataclass validator ``validate_token_ids``
116	    does ``for name in text_config: getattr(text_config, name)`` — unconditional
117	    iteration, raw ``getattr``. The proxy's ``__iter__`` forwards to the real
118	    config's iterator, which yields ``num_kv_shared_layers`` — and the raw
119	    ``getattr`` on the proxy then triggers the raise. Filtering the name out
120	    of ``__iter__`` makes the validator skip it without disturbing ``hasattr``.
121	    """
122	    try:
123	        from unsloth_zoo.temporary_patches.gemma4 import _Gemma4KVSharedSafeProxy
124	    except ImportError:
125	        return
126	
127	    _sentinel = "_unsloth_gemma4_proxy_iter_filtered"
128	    orig_iter = _Gemma4KVSharedSafeProxy.__iter__
129	    if getattr(orig_iter, _sentinel, False):
130	        return
131	
132	    def __iter__(self):  # noqa: ANN001
133	        return iter(
134	            name for name in object.__getattribute__(self, "_real")
135	            if name != "num_kv_shared_layers"
136	        )
137	
138	    setattr(__iter__, _sentinel, True)
139	    _Gemma4KVSharedSafeProxy.__iter__ = __iter__
140	
141	
142	def _generate_for_checkpoint(
143	    checkpoint: str,
144	    prompts: list[dict[str, Any]],
145	    n_completions: int,
146	    max_new_tokens: int,
147	    temperature: float,
148	    top_p: float,
149	    max_seq_length: int,
150	    batch_size: int,
151	    seed: int,
152	    do_sample: bool = True,
153	) -> list[list[str]]:
154	    """Load `checkpoint`, generate K completions per prompt, return per-prompt lists.
155	
156	    Each prompt → list[str] of length n_completions. With ``do_sample=True``
157	    (default) generation matches GRPO sampling (T=1.0, top_p=0.95) so reward_std
158	    here predicts what GRPO sees. With ``do_sample=False`` it decodes greedily
159	    (temperature/top_p ignored) — used by the RFT headroom probe to score the
160	    deterministic baseline each prompt's best-of-K is measured against.
161	    """
162	    # Unsloth must be imported before torch so its monkey-patches take effect.
163	    from unsloth import FastLanguageModel  # noqa: I001
164	    import torch
165	
166	    # Importing unsloth installs unsloth_zoo's Gemma-4 KV-zero proxy. The
167	    # proxy is load-bearing for the cache __init__ workaround (its raising
168	    # __getattr__ on num_kv_shared_layers makes hasattr() return False, which
169	    # bypasses transformers' buggy layer_types[:-0] == [] slice). But under
170	    # transformers 5.9.0 the strict-dataclass validator iterates proxy
171	    # attributes and raw-getattrs each name, hitting the raise. Patch only
172	    # the proxy's __iter__ to skip num_kv_shared_layers — preserves cache
173	    # behaviour, bypasses the validator.
174	    _patch_unsloth_gemma4_proxy_iter()
175	
176	    print(f"[load] {checkpoint}", flush=True)
177	    t0 = time.time()
178	    model, tokenizer = FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained(
179	        model_name=checkpoint,
180	        max_seq_length=max_seq_length,
181	        dtype=None,
182	        load_in_4bit=True,
183	    )
184	    FastLanguageModel.for_inference(model)
185	    print(f"[load] done in {time.time() - t0:.1f}s", flush=True)
186	
187	    # Gemma-4 returns a multimodal `Gemma4Processor` from
188	    # FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained, not a bare tokenizer. The processor's
189	    # __call__ signature is (images, text, videos, ...) and rejects positional
190	    # text — and apply_chat_template lives on the inner .tokenizer for the
191	    # processor case. Resolve once here and use the inner tokenizer for both
192	    # template rendering and tokenization.
193	    inner_tok = getattr(tokenizer, "tokenizer", tokenizer)
194	
195	    # Render prompts once; reuse the tokenized form across the K rollouts.
196	    rendered: list[str] = []
197	    for p in prompts:
198	        rendered.append(
199	            inner_tok.apply_chat_template(
200	                p["prompt_messages"],
201	                tokenize=False,
202	                add_generation_prompt=True,
203	            )
204	        )
205	
206	    completions_per_prompt: list[list[str]] = [[] for _ in prompts]
207	
208	    # Each "rollout pass" generates one completion for each of the N prompts;
209	    # do K passes to get K completions per prompt. Each pass uses a different
210	    # seed so the K samples are independent. Within a pass we batch across
211	    # prompts (batch_size) to amortize the per-call overhead.
212	    for k in range(n_completions):
213	        torch.manual_seed(seed + k)
214	        for batch_start in range(0, len(rendered), batch_size):
215	            batch = rendered[batch_start:batch_start + batch_size]
216	            inputs = inner_tok(
217	                batch,
218	                return_tensors="pt",
219	                padding=True,
220	                truncation=True,
221	                max_length=max_seq_length - max_new_tokens,
222	            ).to(model.device)
223	            gen_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {
224	                "max_new_tokens": max_new_tokens,
225	                "pad_token_id": inner_tok.pad_token_id or inner_tok.eos_token_id,
226	            }
227	            if do_sample:
228	                gen_kwargs.update(do_sample=True, temperature=temperature, top_p=top_p)
229	            else:
230	                gen_kwargs.update(do_sample=False)
231	            with torch.inference_mode():
232	                out = model.generate(**inputs, **gen_kwargs)
233	            # Strip prompt tokens; decode only the new portion.
234	            for j, seq in enumerate(out):
235	                prompt_len = inputs["input_ids"][j].shape[0]
236	                new_tokens = seq[prompt_len:]
237	                text = inner_tok.decode(new_tokens, skip_special_tokens=True)
238	                completions_per_prompt[batch_start + j].append(text)
239	            print(
240	                f"[gen] ckpt={Path(checkpoint).name} pass={k+1}/{n_completions} "
241	                f"batch={batch_start//batch_size + 1}",
242	                flush=True,
243	            )
244	
245	    # Free GPU memory before the next checkpoint loads.
246	    del model
247	    del tokenizer
248	    gc.collect()
249	    torch.cuda.empty_cache()
250	    return completions_per_prompt
251	
252	
253	def _score_per_component(completion: str, gt: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, float]:
254	    """Compute the 4 reward sub-components (2026-05-21 redesign) for one (completion, gt) pair.
255	
256	    Mirrors the current reward_business_logic body: state_transition (graded),
257	    tool_call_f1 (graded, placeholder-stripped), task_completion (active when
258	    terminal_reached), transition_legality (active when valid_transitions
259	    non-empty). chain_propagation / format_compliance / length_band were
260	    dropped in the 2026-05-21 redesign and are no longer reported.
261	    """
262	    from llm_workflow_agents.training.reward_utils import (
263	        extract_state_annotations,
264	        extract_tool_calls,
265	        graded_tool_call_f1,
266	        reached_terminal,
267	        transition_legality_score,
268	    )
269	    from llm_workflow_agents.training.rewards.reward_business_logic import (
270	        _graded_state_match,
271	        _strip_placeholder_args,
272	    )
273	
274	    pred_states = extract_state_annotations(completion)
275	    gt_states = gt.get("state_annotations", [])
276	    r_state = _graded_state_match(pred_states, gt_states)
277	
278	    pred_tools = extract_tool_calls(completion)
279	    gt_tools = _strip_placeholder_args(gt.get("tool_calls", []))
280	    r_tool = graded_tool_call_f1(pred_tools, gt_tools)
281	
282	    terminal = gt.get("terminal_state", "")
283	    if not gt.get("terminal_reached", True):
284	        r_completion = None  # rescaled out (active-component mean)
285	    else:
286	        r_completion = 1.0 if reached_terminal(completion, terminal) else 0.0
287	
288	    valid_transitions = gt.get("valid_transitions", [])
289	    if not valid_transitions:
290	        r_legality = None  # rescaled out (active-component mean)
291	    else:
292	        r_legality = transition_legality_score(pred_states, valid_transitions)
293	
294	    return {
295	        "state_transition": r_state,
296	        "tool_call_f1": r_tool,
297	        "task_completion": r_completion,
298	        "transition_legality": r_legality,
299	    }
300	
301	
302	def _score_and_summarize(
303	    completions_per_prompt: list[list[str]],
304	    prompts: list[dict[str, Any]],
305	) -> dict[str, Any]:
306	    """Score every (prompt, completion) and reduce to per-prompt + aggregate stats."""
307	    from llm_workflow_agents.training.rewards.reward_business_logic import (
308	        reward_business_logic,
309	    )
310	
311	    per_prompt: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
312	    for i, (comps, p) in enumerate(zip(completions_per_prompt, prompts)):
313	        gt = _decode_gt(p["ground_truth"])
314	        rewards = reward_business_logic(
315	            prompts=[""] * len(comps),
316	            completions=comps,
317	            ground_truths=[gt] * len(comps),
318	        )
319	        components = [_score_per_component(c, gt) for c in comps]
320	        unique = len({c.strip() for c in comps})
321	        per_prompt.append({
322	            "prompt_index": i,
323	            "rewards": rewards,
324	            "reward_mean": statistics.fmean(rewards),
325	            "reward_std": statistics.pstdev(rewards) if len(rewards) > 1 else 0.0,
326	            "n_unique_completions": unique,
327	            "completion_lengths": [len(c) for c in comps],
328	            "completions": comps,
329	            "components": components,
330	            "terminal_reached": bool(gt.get("terminal_reached", True)),
331	        })
332	
333	    stds = [r["reward_std"] for r in per_prompt]
334	    means = [r["reward_mean"] for r in per_prompt]
335	    return {
336	        "per_prompt": per_prompt,
337	        "mean_reward_std": statistics.fmean(stds) if stds else 0.0,
338	        "median_reward_std": statistics.median(stds) if stds else 0.0,
339	        "mean_reward": statistics.fmean(means) if means else 0.0,
340	        "frac_collapsed_groups": (
341	            sum(1 for s in stds if s < 0.01) / len(stds) if stds else 0.0
342	        ),
343	        "mean_unique_per_group": (
344	            statistics.fmean(r["n_unique_completions"] for r in per_prompt)
345	            if per_prompt else 0.0
346	        ),
347	    }
348	
349	
350	def main() -> int:
351	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
352	    parser.add_argument(
353	        "--checkpoints", nargs="+", required=True,
354	        help="Two or more SFT checkpoint paths to compare.",
355	    )
356	    parser.add_argument("--data-dir", type=Path, default=Path("data/output/grpo/task_a"))
357	    parser.add_argument("--split", default="validation")
358	    parser.add_argument("--n-prompts", type=int, default=5)
359	    parser.add_argument("--n-completions", type=int, default=4)
360	    parser.add_argument("--max-new-tokens", type=int, default=512)
361	    parser.add_argument("--temperature", type=float, default=1.0)
362	    parser.add_argument("--top-p", type=float, default=0.95)
363	    parser.add_argument("--max-seq-length", type=int, default=8192)
364	    parser.add_argument("--batch-size", type=int, default=4)
365	    parser.add_argument("--seed", type=int, default=42)
366	    parser.add_argument("--output", type=Path, required=True)
367	    args = parser.parse_args()
368	
369	    args.output.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
370	
371	    prompts = _sample_prompts(args.data_dir, args.split, args.n_prompts, args.seed)
372	    print(f"[data] sampled {len(prompts)} prompts from {args.data_dir}/{args.split}.jsonl",
373	          flush=True)
374	
375	    results: dict[str, Any] = {
376	        "config": {
377	            "checkpoints": args.checkpoints,
378	            "n_prompts": len(prompts),
379	            "n_completions": args.n_completions,
380	            "max_new_tokens": args.max_new_tokens,
381	            "temperature": args.temperature,
382	            "top_p": args.top_p,
383	            "seed": args.seed,
384	        },
385	        "by_checkpoint": {},
386	    }
387	
388	    for ckpt in args.checkpoints:
389	        t0 = time.time()
390	        comps = _generate_for_checkpoint(
391	            checkpoint=ckpt,
392	            prompts=prompts,
393	            n_completions=args.n_completions,
394	            max_new_tokens=args.max_new_tokens,
395	            temperature=args.temperature,
396	            top_p=args.top_p,
397	            max_seq_length=args.max_seq_length,
398	            batch_size=args.batch_size,
399	            seed=args.seed,
400	        )
401	        summary = _score_and_summarize(comps, prompts)
402	        summary["wall_time_s"] = round(time.time() - t0, 1)
403	        # All completions are now stored in summary["per_prompt"][i]["completions"];
404	        # no need for a separate sample field.
405	        results["by_checkpoint"][ckpt] = summary
406	
407	        print(
408	            f"\n[summary] {ckpt}\n"
409	            f"  mean_reward_std       = {summary['mean_reward_std']:.4f}\n"
410	            f"  median_reward_std     = {summary['median_reward_std']:.4f}\n"
411	            f"  mean_reward           = {summary['mean_reward']:.4f}\n"
412	            f"  frac_collapsed_groups = {summary['frac_collapsed_groups']:.2f}\n"
413	            f"  mean_unique_per_group = {summary['mean_unique_per_group']:.2f}"
414	            f" / {args.n_completions}\n"
415	            f"  wall_time_s           = {summary['wall_time_s']}",
416	            flush=True,
417	        )
418	
419	    # Print side-by-side comparison table.
420	    print("\n=== Side-by-side ===")
421	    header = f"{'metric':<26} " + " ".join(f"{Path(c).name:>22}" for c in args.checkpoints)
422	    print(header)
423	    for metric in ("mean_reward_std", "median_reward_std", "mean_reward",
424	                   "frac_collapsed_groups", "mean_unique_per_group"):
425	        row = f"{metric:<26} " + " ".join(
426	            f"{results['by_checkpoint'][c][metric]:>22.4f}" for c in args.checkpoints
427	        )
428	        print(row)
429	
430	    args.output.write_text(json.dumps(results, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))
431	    print(f"\n[done] wrote {args.output}", flush=True)
432	    return 0
433	
434	
435	if __name__ == "__main__":
436	    raise SystemExit(main())
